countries.recommended_next_step: Existing regional anchor; use as baseline for next-step comparisons
countries.unique_opportunity: Fab 12i already gives UMC a Southeast Asia manufacturing anchor and customer qualification base.
countries.unique_risk: Additional Singapore expansion can crowd out the next-step question and may face higher cost and resource constraints.
location_level_questions: Which Fab 12i customer workflows still require local customer engineering expansion?
Which activities should remain in Singapore versus be extended into a lower-cost nearby market?
evidence_gaps: Incremental Fab 12i customer support workload by product family
Cost comparison for activities that could be located outside Singapore
priority_movers: Moves up if incremental work must stay close to the fab for customer qualification.
Moves down if partner and customer support work can be handled from Malaysia or Vietnam.

sales_office: Which regional headquarters or anchor accounts still need Singapore-based coverage?
What account coverage should be shifted to non-Singapore markets?
customer_engineering_hub: Which Fab 12i customer engineering issues require on-site escalation in Singapore?
Which support workflows can be distributed to a lower-cost hub?
packaging_osat_partnership: Which wafer-to-package coordination work must stay near Fab 12i?
Which OSAT coordination work can be partnered outside Singapore?
foundry_manufacturing_expansion: What incremental specialty capacity would justify further Singapore manufacturing exposure?
How would additional expansion compare with non-Singapore next-step options?

countries.recommended_next_step: Partner shortlist plus customer overlap analysis; OSAT economics and incentive review
countries.unique_opportunity: Penang and Kulim offer the strongest non-Singapore Packaging, Assembly & Test ecosystem for a partnership-first Fab 12i extension.
countries.unique_risk: Talent competition, partner economics, and incentive details could weaken the near-term business case.
location_level_questions: Penang vs Kulim: which location best supports OSAT/test access and Fab 12i wafer-to-package coordination?
Which partner site has the right logistics, quality systems, and customer access?
evidence_gaps: Named customer overlap for UMC wafers needing Malaysia-based packaging or test
Partner capacity, attach-rate economics, and incentive package detail
priority_movers: Moves up if anchor customers validate wafer-to-package demand through Malaysia.
Moves down if OSAT partner economics or engineering talent availability are weaker than expected.

packaging_osat_partnership: Penang vs Kulim: where is OSAT and testing coordination strongest?
Which OSAT / testing partners should be shortlisted?
Is the right first step partnership, joint development, or customer engineering hub?
How would Fab 12i wafer-to-package support operate across logistics, quality, and escalation?
Which anchor customers or attach-rate assumptions validate the model?
customer_engineering_hub: Which Penang/Kulim customers or partners need FAE and quality coordination tied to Fab 12i output?
Can a customer engineering hub improve packaging/test issue resolution without committing to capex?
sales_office: Which Malaysia-based EMS, OSAT, and electronics customers require direct account coverage?
Would sales coverage be standalone or paired with technical support?
foundry_manufacturing_expansion: Would Malaysia offer a credible specialty manufacturing case beyond OSAT adjacency?
What utility, permitting, and customer qualification hurdles would block a foundry option?

countries.recommended_next_step: Customer engineering validation; EMS and assembly ecosystem relevance review
countries.unique_opportunity: Vietnam is a plausible EMS-linked customer engineering and account operations watchlist market.
countries.unique_risk: The ecosystem is more downstream and assembly-oriented, so it may not yet support a foundry-first or advanced packaging-first thesis.
location_level_questions: Bac Ninh, Ho Chi Minh City, or Hanoi: which location best matches customer engineering and EMS coverage needs?
Which local customers or partners require technical escalation rather than account operations only?
evidence_gaps: Customer overlap with UMC communication, consumer, IoT, and display-related accounts
Depth of local FAE and semiconductor quality support talent
priority_movers: Moves up if EMS-linked customers need local technical support for Fab 12i products.
Moves down if local demand is mostly account operations rather than engineering support.

customer_engineering_hub: Which EMS and assembly ecosystem nodes are relevant to UMC customer support?
How much customer overlap exists with UMC's current product and account base?
Is Vietnam better suited for customer engineering, account operations, or both?
Is technical support talent deep enough for FAE, quality, and design-in support?
sales_office: Which Vietnam electronics and EMS clusters justify local account coverage?
Would a representative office be enough before adding engineering support?
packaging_osat_partnership: Are local assembly and test players mature enough for Fab 12i wafer-to-package coordination?
Which partner capabilities are still missing versus Malaysia?
foundry_manufacturing_expansion: What policy, utility, and qualification gaps make foundry manufacturing a longer-term watchlist option?
What evidence would change Vietnam from support hub to manufacturing diligence?

countries.recommended_next_step: Automotive / industrial customer overlap; specialty process demand validation
countries.unique_opportunity: Thailand is a specialty-process watchlist market tied to automotive, power, industrial, and electronics demand clusters.
countries.unique_risk: Policy ambition and demand signals need proof before moving beyond sales coverage or customer engineering.
location_level_questions: Bangkok/EEC: which customer cluster best supports automotive, power, and industrial electronics coverage?
Which local policy commitments are executable for semiconductor suppliers and customer support?
evidence_gaps: Named UMC customer overlap in automotive, power, and industrial electronics
Implementation detail for semiconductor policy, incentives, talent, and infrastructure
priority_movers: Moves up if automotive and power electronics customers validate specialty-process demand.
Moves down if policy remains aspirational or customer overlap is thin.

sales_office: Which automotive, power, and industrial electronics accounts justify direct sales coverage?
Would sales coverage improve demand sensing for UMC specialty processes?
customer_engineering_hub: Does Thailand require FAE support or mainly regional account coverage?
Which quality or design-in workflows would benefit from local engineering support?
packaging_osat_partnership: Are there packaging, assembly, or test partners relevant to automotive and power electronics?
Would Thailand complement or duplicate Malaysia's packaging/test role?
foundry_manufacturing_expansion: Is the national semiconductor strategy executable enough to support manufacturing diligence?
Which specialty process demand would justify foundry-level review?

countries.recommended_next_step: Support hub service model; talent and escalation workflow validation
countries.unique_opportunity: The Philippines is a support hub and allied supply-chain watchlist option, with account operations and technical escalation potential.
countries.unique_risk: It is not a foundry-first option without stronger manufacturing, utility, and customer evidence.
location_level_questions: New Clark City, Manila, or Calabarzon: which location best fits support operations and supply-chain access?
Which functions should be technical support versus account operations?
evidence_gaps: Customer overlap and escalation workflows tied to Fab 12i products
priority_movers: Moves up if customer support demand and allied supply-chain programs translate into named opportunities.
Moves down if the role is limited to generic back-office support.

customer_engineering_hub: Which support workflows require semiconductor technical depth rather than account operations only?
Can the talent base support FAE coordination, quality triage, and customer engineering escalation?
sales_office: Which customers or government relationships require a local account presence?
Would coverage be better served from Singapore, Malaysia, or the Philippines?
packaging_osat_partnership: Which assembly/test capabilities are relevant to UMC products and Fab 12i output?
Does the partner base support a differentiated role versus Malaysia?
foundry_manufacturing_expansion: What would need to be true on utilities, policy, and customer qualification before foundry diligence?
Is the New Clark City supply-chain security concept relevant to UMC manufacturing or only watchlist evidence?

countries.recommended_next_step: Policy/design ecosystem monitoring; customer demand and partner evidence validation
countries.unique_opportunity: Indonesia is a long-term IC design, policy, and market watchlist option rather than an immediate manufacturing target.
countries.unique_risk: Manufacturing readiness and semiconductor customer density remain less proven than Malaysia, Singapore, or Vietnam.
location_level_questions: Jakarta or Batam: which location best fits design ecosystem, policy engagement, or customer coverage?
Are Arm/Danantara-type initiatives creating UMC-relevant design demand or mainly workforce development?
evidence_gaps: Named fabless, EMS, or end-customer demand tied to UMC process nodes
Evidence of partner maturity for customer engineering or manufacturing collaboration
priority_movers: Moves up if IC design training produces customer demand tied to mature-node foundry services.
Moves down if policy partnerships do not create near-term commercial demand.

sales_office: Which accounts or policy relationships justify a representative presence?
Is market coverage better handled regionally until customer density improves?
customer_engineering_hub: Is there enough semiconductor customer engineering demand beyond design training?
Which language, support, and technical skills would be required?
packaging_osat_partnership: Are any packaging, test, or EMS partners mature enough for UMC-relevant collaboration?
What evidence would move Indonesia beyond watchlist status?
foundry_manufacturing_expansion: What infrastructure, utility, customer, and policy evidence would be required before manufacturing diligence?
Would mature-node demand justify foundry review within the planning horizon?
